title: Conjugations
description: Auto-generated conjugation tables for Japanese verbs and adjectives. Derived from JMdict part-of-speech tags (v1 for ichidan verbs, v5u/v5k/v5g/etc. for godan verbs, adj-i for i-adjectives, adj-na for na-adjectives, plus irregular verbs する/くる) using formal conjugation rules. Because the rules are deterministic and well-documented, these conjugations do not require native-speaker review — any incorrectness indicates a bug in the rule implementation, not in the data.

id.description: JMdict entry ID the conjugation table is derived from.

dictionary_form.description: The dictionary (lemma) form, e.g., 食べる, 行く.

reading.description: Kana reading of the dictionary form.

class.description: Word class used for conjugation (JMdict POS tag or close equivalent).

forms.description: Map from form name to conjugated form in kana. For word classes with no conjugation logic implemented, the forms map may be empty.

display_forms.description: Companion to `forms`: the same conjugated forms with the kanji prefix of the dictionary_form preserved where possible. For pure-kana dictionary forms, display_forms equals forms. For kanji-containing dictionary forms like 行く or 食べる, the common-suffix heuristic replaces the leading kana of each form with the leading kanji of the dictionary form (e.g., 行く → te_form: いって → display: 行って). For adj-na, a class-aware branch replaces the entire reading prefix with the dictionary form, handling compound readings like 大切 (たいせつ) → 大切です where the kanji and kana share no trailing character. Consumers that want to display the kanji-preserved form of a conjugation should prefer display_forms; consumers that want the unambiguous kana form should use forms.
